packages.name: quartz
packages.synopsis: Package quartz is a Quartz-style, in-process job scheduler written entirely with the Go standard library.
packages.doc:
Package quartz is a Quartz-style, in-process job scheduler written entirely
with the Go standard library. It provides jobs, triggers (simple interval and
full cron), a worker pool, listeners, and a pluggable job store.

# Overview

The three central concepts are the Job, the JobDetail, and the Trigger:

  - A Job is the code to run: any type implementing Execute(context.Context)
    error. The JobFunc adapter turns a plain function into a Job.
  - A JobDetail names a job (via a Key), attaches a description and a
    JobDataMap of state, and holds the Job implementation. One JobDetail may
    be fired by many triggers.
  - A Trigger decides when a job fires. Two implementations are provided:
    SimpleTrigger (a start time plus a fixed interval and an optional repeat
    count) and CronTrigger (a full cron expression evaluated in a chosen time
    zone).

A Scheduler ties them together. It stores jobs and triggers in a JobStore,
runs a background loop that fires due triggers, and executes the resulting
work on a bounded pool of worker goroutines.

# Cron expressions

CronTrigger uses ParseCron, which understands six space separated fields
(second, minute, hour, day-of-month, month, day-of-week); a five field
expression without seconds is also accepted. Fields support ranges (1-5),
steps (*/15 and 10-30/5), lists (1,3,5), names (JAN..DEC and SUN..SAT), and
the * and ? wildcards. See CronExpression for the exact grammar and the day
matching rules.

# Deterministic time

Every fire-time decision flows through an injectable clock. Triggers compute
their next fire time as a pure function of a supplied "now", and the
Scheduler reads time through Options.Clock. Tests can therefore drive the
scheduler with a virtual clock and call Scheduler.ProcessDue directly,
without sleeping or depending on the wall clock.

# Lifecycle and control

Jobs and triggers can be scheduled, unscheduled, paused, and resumed
individually or by job. Start launches the worker pool and background loop;
Shutdown stops them, optionally draining in-flight work. Triggers that fire
late are reconciled through a configurable misfire policy.

# Listeners

JobListener and TriggerListener expose hooks around each execution:
TriggerListener can even veto a job for a particular fire. Embed
BaseJobListener or BaseTriggerListener to implement only the callbacks you
need.

# Concurrency

The Scheduler, MemoryJobStore, and all exported scheduler methods are safe
for concurrent use. Trigger implementations are mutated only by the scheduler
and are not intended to be advanced from multiple goroutines simultaneously.

# Example

The following schedules a job to run every 15 seconds:

	s := quartz.NewScheduler(quartz.Options{Concurrency: 4})
	job := quartz.NewJobDetail(quartz.NewKey("report"), quartz.JobFunc(
		func(ctx context.Context) error { return nil }))
	trig := quartz.NewSimpleTrigger(
		quartz.NewKey("report-trigger"), job.Key(),
		time.Now(), 15*time.Second, quartz.RepeatForever)
	_ = s.ScheduleJob(job, trig)
	_ = s.Start()
	defer s.Shutdown(true)

consts.doc: DefaultGroup is used when a Key is created without an explicit group.

RepeatForever is used as the repeat count of a SimpleTrigger that should keep
firing without a fixed limit.

types.name: AnnualCalendar
types.doc:
AnnualCalendar excludes a specific month and day that recurs every year (for
example December 25). The year is ignored when matching. Exclusions have day
granularity and the date is determined in the calendar's location.

funcs.name: NewAnnualCalendar
funcs.doc: NewAnnualCalendar returns an AnnualCalendar with no days excluded.

methods.name: IsTimeIncluded
methods.doc:
IsTimeIncluded reports whether t's month and day are not excluded and the base
Calendar also includes t.

methods.name: NextIncludedTime
methods.doc:
NextIncludedTime returns the earliest instant at or after t that does not fall
on an excluded annual date and is allowed by the base Calendar.

methods.name: SetDayExcluded
methods.signature: func (c *AnnualCalendar) SetDayExcluded(month time.Month, day int, excluded bool)
methods.doc: SetDayExcluded sets whether the given month and day are excluded every year.

types.name: BaseCalendar
types.doc:
BaseCalendar provides the state and delegation shared by every Calendar
implementation in this package. It stores an optional base Calendar and an
optional evaluation location, and is meant to be embedded. A BaseCalendar
excludes no instant of its own, so used directly it behaves as a pass-through
to its base (or includes everything when it has no base). Embedding types
override IsTimeIncluded and NextIncludedTime to add their own exclusion rule
while still consulting the base through the helpers it provides.

methods.name: GetBaseCalendar
methods.doc: GetBaseCalendar returns the base Calendar, or nil when none is set.

methods.name: IsTimeIncluded
methods.doc:
IsTimeIncluded reports whether t is included. A BaseCalendar contributes no
exclusion of its own, so the result is determined solely by its base Calendar
(or true when there is no base).

methods.name: Location
methods.doc:
Location returns the evaluation time zone, or nil when instants are evaluated
in their own location.

methods.name: NextIncludedTime
methods.doc:
NextIncludedTime returns the next instant at or after t allowed by the base
Calendar, or t itself when there is no base.

methods.name: SetBaseCalendar
methods.doc:
SetBaseCalendar sets the base Calendar consulted in addition to this one. A
nil base clears any existing base.

methods.name: SetLocation
methods.doc:
SetLocation sets the time zone in which wall-clock exclusions (day, weekday,
time-of-day and cron rules) are evaluated. A nil location means each instant
is evaluated in its own location.

types.name: BaseJobListener
types.signature: type BaseJobListener struct{ ListenerName string }
types.doc:
BaseJobListener is a no-op JobListener that can be embedded to implement only
the methods of interest.

methods.name: JobToBeExecuted
methods.doc: JobToBeExecuted implements JobListener.

methods.name: JobWasExecuted
methods.doc: JobWasExecuted implements JobListener.

methods.name: Name
methods.doc: Name implements JobListener.

types.name: BaseTriggerListener
types.signature: type BaseTriggerListener struct{ ListenerName string }
types.doc:
BaseTriggerListener is a no-op TriggerListener that can be embedded to
implement only the methods of interest.

methods.name: Name
methods.doc: Name implements TriggerListener.

methods.name: TriggerComplete
methods.doc: TriggerComplete implements TriggerListener.

methods.name: TriggerFired
methods.doc: TriggerFired implements TriggerListener.

methods.name: VetoJobExecution
methods.doc: VetoJobExecution implements TriggerListener and never vetoes.

types.name: Calendar
types.doc:
Calendar excludes selected instants from a trigger's fire schedule. Calendars
are additive: a Calendar may wrap a base Calendar so that an instant is
considered included only when it is included by this Calendar and by its base,
letting several exclusion rules compose into one. Implementations must be safe
for use by a single scheduler goroutine at a time, matching the concurrency
contract of Trigger.

types.name: CalendarIntervalScheduleBuilder
types.doc:
CalendarIntervalScheduleBuilder builds a CalendarIntervalTrigger that advances
by a whole calendar unit (day, week, month, year, or a sub-day duration).
Create one with CalendarIntervalSchedule.

funcs.name: CalendarIntervalSchedule
funcs.doc:
CalendarIntervalSchedule starts building a calendar-interval schedule. The
default is one day.

methods.name: BuildTrigger
methods.signature: func (b *CalendarIntervalScheduleBuilder) BuildTrigger(key, jobKey Key, start, end time.Time, description string) (Trigger, error)
methods.doc: BuildTrigger implements ScheduleBuilder.

methods.name: In
methods.doc:
In sets the time zone used to evaluate calendar advancement and returns the
builder for chaining. A nil location is ignored.

methods.name: WithInterval
methods.doc:
WithInterval sets the interval count and unit and returns the builder for
chaining.

methods.name: WithIntervalInDays
methods.doc: WithIntervalInDays sets a day interval and returns the builder for chaining.

methods.name: WithIntervalInMonths
methods.doc:
WithIntervalInMonths sets a month interval and returns the builder for
chaining.

methods.name: WithIntervalInWeeks
methods.doc: WithIntervalInWeeks sets a week interval and returns the builder for chaining.

methods.name: WithIntervalInYears
methods.doc: WithIntervalInYears sets a year interval and returns the builder for chaining.

methods.name: WithMisfirePolicy
methods.doc:
WithMisfirePolicy sets the misfire policy and returns the builder for
chaining.

types.name: CalendarIntervalTrigger
types.doc:
CalendarIntervalTrigger fires starting at a given time and then repeats at a
fixed count of calendar units. Because it advances by calendar units rather
than a fixed duration, successive fires honor the varying length of months and
years and remain aligned to wall-clock time across daylight saving
transitions. Advancement is computed in the trigger's location (time.UTC by
default): day, week, month and year steps use time.Time.AddDate while
sub-day steps (second, minute, hour) are added as an absolute duration with
time.Time.Add.

Its next, prev and timesTriggered state fields mirror SimpleTrigger so a SQL
job store can persist and restore an in-flight trigger.

funcs.name: NewCalendarIntervalTrigger
funcs.signature: func NewCalendarIntervalTrigger(key, jobKey Key, start time.Time, unit IntervalUnit, count int) *CalendarIntervalTrigger
funcs.doc:
NewCalendarIntervalTrigger builds a CalendarIntervalTrigger that first fires at
start and then repeats every count units. A count that is zero or negative
yields a trigger that fires only once. The trigger evaluates advancement in
time.UTC until changed with In.

methods.name: ComputeFirstFireTime
methods.doc:
ComputeFirstFireTime implements Trigger. It sets the first fire time to the
configured start time, or the zero time when the start time is already past
the end time. The now argument is unused because the first fire is always the
start time.

methods.name: Count
methods.doc: Count returns the number of units advanced between fires.

methods.name: Description
methods.doc: Description implements Trigger.

methods.name: EndTime
methods.doc: EndTime returns the configured end time, or the zero time when unbounded.

methods.name: FireTimeAfter
methods.doc:
FireTimeAfter returns the first scheduled fire time strictly after the given
time, or the zero time when the trigger has no such fire (it is past its end
time or its count is non-positive so it fires only once). It is a pure query
that does not consult or mutate the trigger's running state, mirroring the
original Quartz CalendarIntervalTrigger.getFireTimeAfter(Date). Advancement
uses the same calendar arithmetic as normal firing, so successive fires honor
the varying length of months and years in the trigger's location.

methods.name: In
methods.doc:
In sets the time zone used to evaluate calendar advancement and returns the
trigger. A nil location is ignored, leaving the current location in place.

methods.name: JobKey
methods.doc: JobKey implements Trigger.

methods.name: Key
methods.doc: Key implements Trigger.

methods.name: Location
methods.doc: Location returns the time zone used to evaluate calendar advancement.

methods.name: MisfirePolicy
methods.doc: MisfirePolicy implements Trigger.

methods.name: NextFireTime
methods.doc: NextFireTime implements Trigger.

methods.name: PreserveWallClock
methods.doc:
PreserveWallClock controls how day, week, month and year advancement behaves
across daylight saving transitions and returns the trigger. When enabled the
natural time.Time.AddDate result is used, which keeps the wall-clock
time-of-day fixed (for example a 09:00 local fire stays at 09:00 local even
when the UTC offset changes). When disabled the result is shifted by the
change in UTC offset so the absolute time-of-day (in UTC) is preserved
instead. It has no effect on sub-day units or in zones without daylight
saving. It is disabled by default.

methods.name: PreviousFireTime
methods.doc: PreviousFireTime implements Trigger.

methods.name: StartTime
methods.doc: StartTime returns the configured start time.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns a debug representation of the trigger.

methods.name: TimesTriggered
methods.doc: TimesTriggered returns the number of times the trigger has fired.

methods.name: Triggered
methods.doc:
Triggered implements Trigger. It records the just-fired time, advances the
next fire time by one unit*count step, and returns the new next fire time
(the zero time once the trigger is exhausted by its end time or a
non-positive count).

methods.name: Unit
methods.doc: Unit returns the calendar unit the trigger advances by.

methods.name: UpdateAfterMisfire
methods.doc:
UpdateAfterMisfire implements Trigger. It reschedules the trigger relative to
now according to its misfire policy: MisfireIgnore leaves the next fire time
unchanged, MisfireFireNow sets it to now, and MisfireSmart and
MisfireDoNothing repeatedly advance whole unit*count steps until the next
fire time is strictly after now.

methods.name: WillFireAgain
methods.doc: WillFireAgain implements Trigger.

methods.name: WithDescription
methods.doc: WithDescription sets the trigger description and returns the trigger.

methods.name: WithEndTime
methods.doc:
WithEndTime sets a time after which the trigger will not fire and returns the
trigger. The zero time means the trigger has no end.

methods.name: WithMisfirePolicy
methods.doc: WithMisfirePolicy sets the misfire policy and returns the trigger.

types.name: CalendarTrigger
types.doc:
CalendarTrigger decorates another Trigger with a Calendar so that scheduled
fire times never land on an instant the Calendar excludes. It delegates every
Trigger method to the wrapped trigger but, after the wrapped trigger produces
a next fire time, advances the wrapped trigger past any excluded instant using
the Calendar. The wrapped trigger and any scheduler using it are left
untouched; the exclusion behavior lives entirely in this decorator.

funcs.name: WithCalendar
funcs.doc:
WithCalendar returns a CalendarTrigger that fires like inner but skips every
instant excluded by cal. A nil cal disables filtering and makes the decorator
a transparent pass-through.

methods.name: ComputeFirstFireTime
methods.doc:
ComputeFirstFireTime implements Trigger. It computes the wrapped trigger's
first fire time and then advances past any excluded instant so the first fire
is never on an excluded time.

methods.name: Description
methods.doc: Description implements Trigger by delegating to the wrapped trigger.

methods.name: JobKey
methods.doc: JobKey implements Trigger by delegating to the wrapped trigger.

methods.name: Key
methods.doc: Key implements Trigger by delegating to the wrapped trigger.

methods.name: MisfirePolicy
methods.doc: MisfirePolicy implements Trigger by delegating to the wrapped trigger.

methods.name: NextFireTime
methods.doc:
NextFireTime implements Trigger. It returns the wrapped trigger's next fire
time, which is always an included instant (or the zero time) because every
mutating call re-applies the Calendar filter.

methods.name: PreviousFireTime
methods.doc: PreviousFireTime implements Trigger by delegating to the wrapped trigger.

methods.name: Triggered
methods.doc:
Triggered implements Trigger. It advances the wrapped trigger and then skips
forward over any excluded instant so the returned next fire time is included.

methods.name: UpdateAfterMisfire
methods.doc:
UpdateAfterMisfire implements Trigger. It applies the wrapped trigger's
misfire handling and then re-applies the Calendar filter so the rescheduled
fire time is never on an excluded time.

methods.name: WillFireAgain
methods.doc: WillFireAgain implements Trigger by delegating to the wrapped trigger.

types.name: CronCalendar
types.doc:
CronCalendar excludes every instant that matches a cron expression. It reuses
the package cron parser, so any expression accepted by ParseCron is accepted
here. Matching is performed at one-second granularity in the calendar's
location.

funcs.name: NewCronCalendar
funcs.doc:
NewCronCalendar returns a CronCalendar that excludes instants matching expr.
It returns the error from ParseCron when expr is invalid.

methods.name: IsTimeIncluded
methods.doc:
IsTimeIncluded reports whether t does not match the expression and the base
Calendar also includes t.

methods.name: NextIncludedTime
methods.doc:
NextIncludedTime returns the earliest instant at or after t that does not
match the expression and is allowed by the base Calendar.

types.name: CronExpression
types.doc:
CronExpression is a parsed cron schedule. It supports six space separated
fields:

	second      0-59
	minute      0-59
	hour        0-23
	dayOfMonth  1-31
	month       1-12 or JAN-DEC
	dayOfWeek   0-6  or SUN-SAT (0 and 7 both mean Sunday)

A five field expression (without seconds) is also accepted; a "0" seconds
field is assumed.

Each field supports the following syntax (shown with literal tokens):

	star     every value
	?        no specific value (treated as star; for dayOfMonth/dayOfWeek)
	1-5      an inclusive range
	star/15  a step over the whole range
	10-30/5  a step over a range
	1,3,5    a list of values or ranges
	MON,JAN  three letter names (case insensitive) for month and dayOfWeek

Day matching follows Unix semantics: when both dayOfMonth and dayOfWeek are
restricted (neither * nor ?), a day matches if either field matches;
otherwise only the restricted field applies.

funcs.name: MustParseCron
funcs.doc:
MustParseCron is like ParseCron but panics on error. It is intended for use
with constant expressions in tests and package initialization.

funcs.name: ParseCron
funcs.doc:
ParseCron parses a cron expression. It returns an error describing the first
problem encountered.

methods.name: IsSatisfiedBy
methods.doc:
IsSatisfiedBy reports whether the given time exactly matches the expression,
evaluated in the time's own location and at whole-second granularity
(sub-second components are ignored). It mirrors the original Quartz
CronExpression.isSatisfiedBy(Date) predicate: a time satisfies the expression
when its second, minute, hour and month all match and its day satisfies the
day-of-month / day-of-week rule.

methods.name: Next
methods.doc:
Next returns the first time strictly after the given time that matches the
expression, in the time's own location. It returns the zero time if no match
is found within roughly five years (which indicates an impossible schedule
such as February 30th).

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns the original source expression.

types.name: CronScheduleBuilder
types.doc:
CronScheduleBuilder builds a CronTrigger from a cron expression evaluated in a
chosen time zone. Create one with CronSchedule or a convenience constructor.

funcs.name: CronSchedule
funcs.doc:
CronSchedule starts building a cron schedule from the given expression. The
expression is validated immediately and an error is returned if it is
invalid.

funcs.name: CronScheduleDailyAt
funcs.doc:
CronScheduleDailyAt builds a cron schedule that fires every day at the given
hour (0-23) and minute (0-59).

funcs.name: CronScheduleMonthlyOn
funcs.doc:
CronScheduleMonthlyOn builds a cron schedule that fires every month on the
given day-of-month at the given hour and minute.

funcs.name: CronScheduleWeeklyOn
funcs.signature: func CronScheduleWeeklyOn(day time.Weekday, hour, minute int) *CronScheduleBuilder
funcs.doc:
CronScheduleWeeklyOn builds a cron schedule that fires every week on the given
weekday at the given hour and minute.

methods.name: BuildTrigger
methods.signature: func (b *CronScheduleBuilder) BuildTrigger(key, jobKey Key, start, end time.Time, description string) (Trigger, error)
methods.doc: BuildTrigger implements ScheduleBuilder.

methods.name: In
methods.doc:
In sets the time zone used to evaluate the cron expression and returns the
builder for chaining. A nil location is ignored.

methods.name: WithMisfirePolicy
methods.doc:
WithMisfirePolicy sets the misfire policy and returns the builder for
chaining.

types.name: CronTrigger
types.doc:
CronTrigger fires according to a CronExpression, evaluated in a configurable
time zone. Fire times are never before the trigger's start time nor after its
optional end time.

funcs.name: NewCronTrigger
funcs.signature: func NewCronTrigger(key, jobKey, expr string) (*CronTrigger, error)
funcs.doc:
NewCronTrigger builds a CronTrigger from a cron expression string. The
expression is evaluated in UTC unless changed with In. It returns an error if
the expression is invalid.

funcs.name: NewCronTriggerWithKeys
funcs.signature: func NewCronTriggerWithKeys(key, jobKey Key, expr string) (*CronTrigger, error)
funcs.doc: NewCronTriggerWithKeys is like NewCronTrigger but takes fully qualified keys.

methods.name: ComputeFirstFireTime
methods.doc: ComputeFirstFireTime implements Trigger.

methods.name: Description
methods.doc: Description implements Trigger.

methods.name: EndingAt
methods.doc: EndingAt sets the latest time the trigger may fire and returns it.

methods.name: Expression
methods.doc: Expression returns the parsed cron expression.

methods.name: In
methods.doc:
In sets the time zone used to evaluate the cron expression and returns the
trigger. A nil location is ignored.

methods.name: JobKey
methods.doc: JobKey implements Trigger.

methods.name: Key
methods.doc: Key implements Trigger.

methods.name: MisfirePolicy
methods.doc: MisfirePolicy implements Trigger.

methods.name: NextFireTime
methods.doc: NextFireTime implements Trigger.

methods.name: PreviousFireTime
methods.doc: PreviousFireTime implements Trigger.

methods.name: StartingAt
methods.doc: StartingAt sets the earliest time the trigger may fire and returns it.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns a debug representation of the trigger.

methods.name: Triggered
methods.doc: Triggered implements Trigger.

methods.name: UpdateAfterMisfire
methods.doc: UpdateAfterMisfire implements Trigger.

methods.name: WillFireAgain
methods.doc: WillFireAgain implements Trigger.

methods.name: WithDescription
methods.doc: WithDescription sets the trigger description and returns the trigger.

methods.name: WithMisfirePolicy
methods.doc: WithMisfirePolicy sets the misfire policy and returns the trigger.

types.name: DailyCalendar
types.doc:
DailyCalendar excludes a wall-clock time-of-day range that repeats every day.
By default the range is the excluded window; Invert flips the sense so that
only instants inside the range are included. The range may wrap past midnight
(when its start is later than its end). The range is evaluated in the
calendar's location.

funcs.name: NewDailyCalendar
funcs.doc:
NewDailyCalendar returns a DailyCalendar that excludes the inclusive
wall-clock range [start, end] every day. When start is later than end the
range wraps across midnight. It reuses the package TimeOfDay type; the range
is defined by each value's hour, minute and second.

methods.name: Invert
methods.doc:
Invert flips whether the configured range is the excluded window or the only
included window, and returns the calendar so the call can be chained.

methods.name: IsTimeIncluded
methods.doc:
IsTimeIncluded reports whether t is included by the time-of-day rule and by
the base Calendar.

methods.name: NextIncludedTime
methods.doc:
NextIncludedTime returns the earliest instant at or after t whose time of day
is allowed and that the base Calendar also allows.

types.name: DailyTimeIntervalScheduleBuilder
types.doc:
DailyTimeIntervalScheduleBuilder builds a DailyTimeIntervalTrigger that fires
repeatedly within a daily time window on selected days of the week. Create one
with DailyTimeIntervalSchedule.

funcs.name: DailyTimeIntervalSchedule
funcs.doc:
DailyTimeIntervalSchedule starts building a daily-time-interval schedule. The
default is a one-minute interval spanning the whole day, every day.

methods.name: BuildTrigger
methods.signature: func (b *DailyTimeIntervalScheduleBuilder) BuildTrigger(key, jobKey Key, start, end time.Time, description string) (Trigger, error)
methods.doc: BuildTrigger implements ScheduleBuilder.

methods.name: EndingDailyAt
methods.doc:
EndingDailyAt sets the latest time-of-day the trigger may fire and returns the
builder for chaining.

methods.name: In
methods.doc:
In sets the time zone used to evaluate the daily window and returns the
builder for chaining. A nil location is ignored.

methods.name: OnDaysOfWeek
methods.doc:
OnDaysOfWeek restricts firing to the given weekdays and returns the builder
for chaining. Passing no days leaves the current selection unchanged.

methods.name: OnEveryDay
methods.doc:
OnEveryDay allows firing on all seven days of the week and returns the builder
for chaining.

methods.name: OnMondayThroughFriday
methods.doc:
OnMondayThroughFriday restricts firing to weekdays and returns the builder for
chaining.

methods.name: OnSaturdayAndSunday
methods.doc:
OnSaturdayAndSunday restricts firing to the weekend and returns the builder
for chaining.

methods.name: StartingDailyAt
methods.doc:
StartingDailyAt sets the earliest time-of-day the trigger may fire and returns
the builder for chaining.

methods.name: WithInterval
methods.doc:
WithInterval sets the interval count and (sub-day) unit and returns the
builder for chaining.

methods.name: WithIntervalInHours
methods.doc:
WithIntervalInHours sets an hours interval and returns the builder for
chaining.

methods.name: WithIntervalInMinutes
methods.doc:
WithIntervalInMinutes sets a minutes interval and returns the builder for
chaining.

methods.name: WithIntervalInSeconds
methods.doc:
WithIntervalInSeconds sets a seconds interval and returns the builder for
chaining.

methods.name: WithMisfirePolicy
methods.doc:
WithMisfirePolicy sets the misfire policy and returns the builder for
chaining.

methods.name: WithRepeatCount
methods.doc:
WithRepeatCount caps the number of fires per day and returns the builder for
chaining. Without it the trigger repeats for the whole window.

types.name: DailyTimeIntervalTrigger
types.doc:
DailyTimeIntervalTrigger fires on a fixed sub-day interval within a daily
time-of-day window, but only on a selected set of weekdays. Within each
active day it starts at the window's start time-of-day and steps forward by
count units (seconds, minutes or hours) as long as the next step does not
pass the window's end time-of-day; once it would pass the end it rolls to the
start time-of-day of the next active day. All arithmetic is performed in the
trigger's location (time.UTC by default) so the window boundaries are
wall-clock times that stay fixed across daylight saving transitions.

The trigger reuses IntervalUnit from CalendarIntervalTrigger (restricted to
IntervalSecond, IntervalMinute and IntervalHour) and the RepeatForever
sentinel from SimpleTrigger. A repeat count of N produces N+1 total fires;
RepeatForever fires without a fixed limit. Its startTime and endTime bound
the trigger absolutely, independent of the daily window.

Its next, prev and timesTriggered state fields mirror SimpleTrigger and
CalendarIntervalTrigger so a SQL job store can persist and restore an
in-flight trigger.

funcs.name: NewDailyTimeIntervalTrigger
funcs.signature: func NewDailyTimeIntervalTrigger(key, jobKey Key, start TimeOfDay, end TimeOfDay, unit IntervalUnit, count int) *DailyTimeIntervalTrigger
funcs.doc:
NewDailyTimeIntervalTrigger builds a DailyTimeIntervalTrigger whose daily
firing window runs from start to end (both wall-clock times-of-day), stepping
by count of the given unit. The unit should be one of IntervalSecond,
IntervalMinute or IntervalHour; other units yield a zero step and the trigger
then fires once per active day at the window start. By default the trigger is
active on all seven weekdays, evaluates times in time.UTC, and repeats
forever; use the fluent builders to change these. The window must have end
no earlier than start, otherwise the trigger never fires.

methods.name: ComputeFirstFireTime
methods.doc:
ComputeFirstFireTime implements Trigger. It finds the first in-window slot at
or after the later of the reference clock now and the configured start time,
honoring the absolute end time. It returns the zero time when the trigger
will never fire.

methods.name: Count
methods.doc: Count returns the number of units advanced between fires within a day.

methods.name: DaysOfWeek
methods.doc: DaysOfWeek returns a copy of the weekdays on which the trigger is active.

methods.name: Description
methods.doc: Description implements Trigger.

methods.name: EndTime
methods.doc:
EndTime returns the configured absolute end time, or the zero time when
unbounded.

methods.name: EndTimeOfDay
methods.doc: EndTimeOfDay returns the end of the daily firing window.

methods.name: EndingAt
methods.doc:
EndingAt sets the absolute time after which the trigger will not fire and
returns the trigger. The zero time means the trigger has no end.

methods.name: In
methods.doc:
In sets the time zone used to evaluate the daily window and returns the
trigger. A nil location is ignored, leaving the current location in place.

methods.name: JobKey
methods.doc: JobKey implements Trigger.

methods.name: Key
methods.doc: Key implements Trigger.

methods.name: Location
methods.doc: Location returns the time zone used to evaluate the daily window.

methods.name: MisfirePolicy
methods.doc: MisfirePolicy implements Trigger.

methods.name: NextFireTime
methods.doc: NextFireTime implements Trigger.

methods.name: OnDaysOfWeek
methods.doc:
OnDaysOfWeek restricts the trigger to fire only on the given weekdays and
returns the trigger. Calling it with no arguments leaves the current set
unchanged (the default is all seven weekdays). The supplied slice is copied
so later external mutation does not leak in.

methods.name: PreviousFireTime
methods.doc: PreviousFireTime implements Trigger.

methods.name: RepeatCount
methods.doc: RepeatCount returns the configured repeat count.

methods.name: StartTime
methods.doc:
StartTime returns the configured absolute start time, or the zero time when
unbounded.

methods.name: StartTimeOfDay
methods.doc: StartTimeOfDay returns the start of the daily firing window.

methods.name: StartingAt
methods.doc:
StartingAt sets the absolute time before which the trigger will not fire and
returns the trigger. The zero time means the trigger has no lower bound
beyond the reference clock passed to ComputeFirstFireTime.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns a debug representation of the trigger.

methods.name: TimesTriggered
methods.doc: TimesTriggered returns the number of times the trigger has fired.

methods.name: Triggered
methods.doc:
Triggered implements Trigger. It records the just-fired time, advances to the
next in-window slot (or the start of the next active day), and returns the
new next fire time (the zero time once the trigger is exhausted by its repeat
count or end time).

methods.name: Unit
methods.doc: Unit returns the sub-day interval unit the trigger advances by within a day.

methods.name: UpdateAfterMisfire
methods.doc:
UpdateAfterMisfire implements Trigger. It reschedules the trigger relative to
now according to its misfire policy, using the same dispatch as the other
triggers: MisfireIgnore leaves the next fire time unchanged, MisfireFireNow
sets it to now (while repeats remain), and MisfireSmart and MisfireDoNothing
advance slot by slot until the next fire time is strictly after now.

methods.name: WillFireAgain
methods.doc: WillFireAgain implements Trigger.

methods.name: WithDescription
methods.doc: WithDescription sets the trigger description and returns the trigger.

methods.name: WithMisfirePolicy
methods.doc: WithMisfirePolicy sets the misfire policy and returns the trigger.

methods.name: WithRepeatCount
methods.doc:
WithRepeatCount sets the number of repeats after the initial fire and returns
the trigger. A count of N yields N+1 total fires; RepeatForever fires without
a fixed limit.

types.name: HolidayCalendar
types.doc:
HolidayCalendar excludes whole calendar days. Exclusions have day
granularity: any instant that falls on an excluded date is excluded
regardless of its time of day. Dates are compared in the calendar's
evaluation location.

funcs.name: NewHolidayCalendar
funcs.doc: NewHolidayCalendar returns a HolidayCalendar with no excluded dates.

methods.name: AddExcludedDate
methods.doc:
AddExcludedDate excludes the calendar day on which t falls. The time of day of
t is ignored.

methods.name: IsTimeIncluded
methods.doc:
IsTimeIncluded reports whether t is included, that is whether its day is not
excluded and the base Calendar also includes it.

methods.name: NextIncludedTime
methods.doc:
NextIncludedTime returns the earliest instant at or after t that is not on an
excluded day and is allowed by the base Calendar.

methods.name: RemoveExcludedDate
methods.doc:
RemoveExcludedDate stops excluding the calendar day on which t falls. It is a
no-op when that day was not excluded.

types.name: IntervalUnit
types.signature: type IntervalUnit int
types.doc:
IntervalUnit identifies the calendar unit by which a CalendarIntervalTrigger
advances between fires. Unlike a fixed time.Duration, calendar units honor the
varying length of months and years as well as daylight saving transitions.

types.name: Job
types.doc:
Job is the unit of work executed by the scheduler. Implementations must be
safe for concurrent use if the same instance is shared across multiple
JobDetails or triggers. The provided context is cancelled when the scheduler
is shutting down without waiting for the job to finish.

types.name: JobBuilder

funcs.name: NewJob
funcs.doc:
NewJob starts building a JobDetail. Attach the work with OfJob and, if
desired, an identity with WithIdentity before calling Build.

methods.name: Build
methods.doc:
Build produces the configured JobDetail. It returns an error if no Job was
supplied. When no identity was set a process-unique name is generated in the
default group.

methods.name: OfJob
methods.doc:
OfJob sets the concrete Job implementation the detail will run and returns the
builder for chaining.

methods.name: StoreDurably
methods.doc:
StoreDurably sets whether the job is retained by the store even when no
triggers reference it and returns the builder for chaining.

methods.name: UsingJobData
methods.doc:
UsingJobData adds a single key/value pair to the job's JobDataMap and returns
the builder for chaining.

methods.name: UsingJobDataMap
methods.doc:
UsingJobDataMap merges every entry of the given map into the job's data and
returns the builder for chaining.

methods.name: WithDescription
methods.doc:
WithDescription sets the job's human readable description and returns the
builder for chaining.

methods.name: WithIdentity
methods.doc: WithIdentity sets the job's key and returns the builder for chaining.

methods.name: WithIdentityName
methods.doc:
WithIdentityName sets the job's key to the given name in the default group and
returns the builder for chaining.

methods.name: WithIdentityNameGroup
methods.doc:
WithIdentityNameGroup sets the job's key to the given name and group and
returns the builder for chaining.

types.name: JobDataMap
types.signature: type JobDataMap map[string]any
types.doc:
JobDataMap is a string-keyed bag of state passed to a Job at execution time.
It is carried both by a JobDetail and, after merging, by every trigger fire.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc:
Clone returns a shallow copy of the map so callers can mutate it without
affecting the original.

methods.name: GetInt
methods.doc:
GetInt returns the value at key as an int and whether it was present and of
the expected type.

methods.name: GetString
methods.doc:
GetString returns the value at key as a string and whether it was present and
of the expected type.

types.name: JobDetail
types.doc:
JobDetail is the definition of a job: its identity, an optional description,
the concrete Job implementation to run, and a JobDataMap of state. A single
JobDetail may be fired by multiple triggers.

funcs.name: NewJobDetail
funcs.doc: NewJobDetail constructs a JobDetail for the given key and Job.

methods.name: Data
methods.doc: Data returns the JobDataMap associated with the job.

methods.name: Description
methods.doc: Description returns the job's description.

methods.name: Durable
methods.doc:
Durable marks the job as durable, meaning it is retained by the store even
when no triggers reference it. Non-durable jobs are removed once their last
trigger is unscheduled.

methods.name: IsDurable
methods.doc: IsDurable reports whether the job is durable.

methods.name: Job
methods.doc: Job returns the concrete Job implementation.

methods.name: Key
methods.doc: Key returns the job's identity.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns a debug representation of the JobDetail.

methods.name: WithData
methods.doc:
WithData sets the JobDataMap and returns the detail for chaining. The map is
cloned so later external mutation does not leak in.

methods.name: WithDescription
methods.doc:
WithDescription sets a human readable description and returns the detail for
chaining.

types.name: JobExecutionContext
types.doc:
JobExecutionContext carries the state of a single job execution as it flows
through listeners and the Job itself.

types.name: JobFunc
types.signature: type JobFunc func(ctx context.Context) error
types.doc: JobFunc adapts an ordinary function to the Job interface.

methods.name: Execute
methods.doc: Execute implements the Job interface.

types.name: JobListener
types.doc:
JobListener receives notifications about the lifecycle of job executions. All
methods are invoked synchronously on the worker goroutine; keep them fast.

types.name: JobStore
types.doc:
JobStore persists JobDetails and Triggers. Implementations must be safe for
concurrent use. The in-memory implementation is MemoryJobStore; a database
backed store can be added by implementing this interface.

types.name: Key
types.doc:
Key uniquely identifies a job or trigger within a group. The zero Key is not
valid; use NewKey to construct one. When a group is omitted it defaults to
DefaultGroup.

funcs.name: MatchKeys
funcs.doc:
MatchKeys returns the subset of keys that satisfy the matcher, preserving
their input order. A nil matcher returns all keys.

funcs.name: NewKey
funcs.doc: NewKey builds a Key in the DefaultGroup.

funcs.name: NewKeyInGroup
funcs.doc:
NewKeyInGroup builds a Key in the given group. An empty group is replaced
with DefaultGroup.

methods.name: IsZero
methods.doc: IsZero reports whether the Key carries no name.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns the "group.name" representation of the Key.

types.name: MatchFunc
types.signature: type MatchFunc func(key Key) bool
types.doc: MatchFunc adapts an ordinary function to the Matcher interface.

methods.name: Matches
methods.doc: Matches implements the Matcher interface.

types.name: Matcher
types.doc:
Matcher reports whether a Key satisfies some criterion. Implementations must
be safe for concurrent use.

funcs.name: AndMatcher
funcs.doc:
AndMatcher returns a Matcher that matches a key only when every supplied
matcher matches it. With no matchers it matches everything.

funcs.name: AnythingMatcher
funcs.doc: AnythingMatcher returns a Matcher that matches every key.

funcs.name: GroupContains
funcs.doc: GroupContains returns a Matcher that matches keys whose group contains sub.

funcs.name: GroupEndsWith
funcs.doc:
GroupEndsWith returns a Matcher that matches keys whose group ends with
suffix.

funcs.name: GroupEquals
funcs.doc: GroupEquals returns a Matcher that matches keys whose group equals group.

funcs.name: GroupStartsWith
funcs.doc:
GroupStartsWith returns a Matcher that matches keys whose group begins with
prefix.

funcs.name: KeyEquals
funcs.doc:
KeyEquals returns a Matcher that matches exactly the given key (both name and
group).

funcs.name: NameContains
funcs.doc: NameContains returns a Matcher that matches keys whose name contains sub.

funcs.name: NameEndsWith
funcs.doc: NameEndsWith returns a Matcher that matches keys whose name ends with suffix.

funcs.name: NameEquals
funcs.doc:
NameEquals returns a Matcher that matches keys whose name equals name,
regardless of group.

funcs.name: NameStartsWith
funcs.doc:
NameStartsWith returns a Matcher that matches keys whose name begins with
prefix.

funcs.name: NotMatcher
funcs.doc:
NotMatcher returns a Matcher that inverts the result of the given matcher. A
nil matcher is treated as matching everything, so its negation matches
nothing.

funcs.name: OrMatcher
funcs.doc:
OrMatcher returns a Matcher that matches a key when at least one supplied
matcher matches it. With no matchers it matches nothing.

types.name: MemoryJobStore
types.doc:
MemoryJobStore is an in-memory, mutex protected JobStore suitable for a
single process scheduler.

funcs.name: NewMemoryJobStore
funcs.doc: NewMemoryJobStore constructs an empty in-memory store.

methods.name: AcquireNextTriggers
methods.doc: AcquireNextTriggers implements JobStore.

methods.name: GetJob
methods.doc: GetJob implements JobStore.

methods.name: GetTrigger
methods.doc: GetTrigger implements JobStore.

methods.name: JobKeys
methods.doc: JobKeys implements JobStore.

methods.name: RemoveJob
methods.doc: RemoveJob implements JobStore.

methods.name: RemoveTrigger
methods.doc: RemoveTrigger implements JobStore.

methods.name: SetTriggerState
methods.signature: func (s *MemoryJobStore) SetTriggerState(key Key, state TriggerState) error
methods.doc: SetTriggerState implements JobStore.

methods.name: StoreJob
methods.doc: StoreJob implements JobStore.

methods.name: StoreTrigger
methods.signature: func (s *MemoryJobStore) StoreTrigger(trigger Trigger, state TriggerState, replace bool) error
methods.doc: StoreTrigger implements JobStore.

methods.name: TriggerKeys
methods.doc: TriggerKeys implements JobStore.

methods.name: TriggersForJob
methods.doc: TriggersForJob implements JobStore.

types.name: MisfirePolicy
types.signature: type MisfirePolicy int
types.doc:
MisfirePolicy determines what a trigger does when the scheduler was unable to
fire it on time (for example while paused or under load).

types.name: MonthlyCalendar
types.doc:
MonthlyCalendar excludes numbered days of the month (1 through 31). Exclusions
have day granularity and the day of month is determined in the calendar's
location. Excluding a day that a given month does not have simply never
matches in that month.

funcs.name: NewMonthlyCalendar
funcs.doc: NewMonthlyCalendar returns a MonthlyCalendar with no days excluded.

methods.name: IsTimeIncluded
methods.doc:
IsTimeIncluded reports whether t's day of month is not excluded and the base
Calendar also includes t.

methods.name: NextIncludedTime
methods.doc:
NextIncludedTime returns the earliest instant at or after t that falls on a
non-excluded day of month and is allowed by the base Calendar.

methods.name: SetDayExcluded
methods.doc:
SetDayExcluded sets whether the given day of month is excluded. Days outside
the range 1 through 31 are ignored.

types.name: Options
types.doc: Options configures a Scheduler.

types.name: SQLJobStore
types.doc:
SQLJobStore is a persistent JobStore backed solely by the standard library
database/sql package. The caller supplies an already configured *sql.DB; no
third-party driver is imported by this package. Jobs are persisted by type
name plus a gob encoded JobDataMap and reconstructed through the factory
registry (see RegisterJobType); triggers are persisted by serializing the
unexported state of the four supported concrete Trigger implementations. All
methods take the store mutex so that operations observe a single,
connection-pool-agnostic order.

funcs.name: NewSQLJobStore
funcs.doc:
NewSQLJobStore constructs a SQLJobStore over the given *sql.DB and options.
It does not touch the database; call CreateSchema to create the backing
tables. The db may be nil only when the store is used solely for the
stateless (de)serialization helpers.

methods.name: AcquireNextTriggers
methods.doc:
AcquireNextTriggers implements JobStore. It returns up to maxCount triggers in
the NORMAL state whose next fire time is at or before noLaterThan, ordered by
fire time. A maxCount of zero or less imposes no limit. It does not mutate any
state.

methods.name: CreateSchema
methods.doc:
CreateSchema creates the jobs and triggers tables if they do not already
exist. It is idempotent and safe to call on every startup.

methods.name: GetJob
methods.doc:
GetJob implements JobStore. It reconstructs the Job through the registered
factory (see RegisterJobType) and rehydrates its JobDataMap. It returns
ErrJobNotFound when no row exists.

methods.name: GetTrigger
methods.doc:
GetTrigger implements JobStore. It returns ErrTriggerNotFound when no row
exists.

methods.name: JobKeys
methods.doc: JobKeys implements JobStore. Keys are ordered by group then name.

methods.name: RemoveJob
methods.doc: RemoveJob implements JobStore. It reports whether a job row was deleted.

methods.name: RemoveTrigger
methods.doc:
RemoveTrigger implements JobStore. It reports whether a trigger row was
deleted.

methods.name: SetTriggerState
methods.signature: func (s *SQLJobStore) SetTriggerState(key Key, state TriggerState) error
methods.doc:
SetTriggerState implements JobStore. It returns ErrTriggerNotFound when no
row matches the key.

methods.name: StoreJob
methods.doc:
StoreJob implements JobStore. It persists the JobDetail's identity, type
name, and gob encoded JobDataMap. If replace is false and a job with the same
key already exists, ErrJobExists is returned.

methods.name: StoreTrigger
methods.signature: func (s *SQLJobStore) StoreTrigger(trigger Trigger, state TriggerState, replace bool) error
methods.doc:
StoreTrigger implements JobStore. It serializes the trigger's concrete state
and persists it in the given state. If replace is false and a trigger with
the same key already exists, ErrTriggerExists is returned.

methods.name: TriggerKeys
methods.doc: TriggerKeys implements JobStore. Keys are ordered by group then name.

methods.name: TriggersForJob
methods.doc:
TriggersForJob implements JobStore. It returns every stored trigger that
references the given job, ordered by group then name.

types.name: SQLJobStoreOptions
types.doc: SQLJobStoreOptions configures a SQLJobStore.

types.name: ScheduleBuilder
types.doc:
ScheduleBuilder is implemented by the schedule builders (simple, cron,
calendar-interval and daily-time-interval). A TriggerBuilder combines a
ScheduleBuilder with an identity, job reference and start/end window to
produce a concrete Trigger.

types.name: Scheduler
types.doc:
Scheduler schedules jobs, fires their triggers, and runs the resulting work
on a bounded worker pool. It is safe for concurrent use.

funcs.name: NewScheduler
funcs.doc:
NewScheduler constructs a Scheduler from Options, applying defaults for any
zero valued field.

methods.name: AddJob
methods.doc: AddJob stores a durable job without any trigger.

methods.name: AddJobListener
methods.doc: AddJobListener registers a JobListener. It must be called before Start.

methods.name: AddTriggerListener
methods.doc:
AddTriggerListener registers a TriggerListener. It must be called before
Start.

methods.name: DeleteJob
methods.doc: DeleteJob removes a job and all of its triggers.

methods.name: IsStarted
methods.doc: IsStarted reports whether the scheduler is running.

methods.name: Now
methods.doc: Now returns the scheduler's current time using its injected clock.

methods.name: PauseJob
methods.doc: PauseJob pauses all triggers of a job.

methods.name: PauseTrigger
methods.doc:
PauseTrigger sets a trigger to the paused state so it will not fire until
resumed.

methods.name: ProcessDue
methods.doc:
ProcessDue fires every trigger that is due at the scheduler's current time.
It is called by the background loop and may also be invoked directly to drive
the scheduler deterministically in tests using an injected clock. It returns
the number of triggers fired.

methods.name: ResumeJob
methods.doc: ResumeJob resumes all triggers of a job.

methods.name: ResumeTrigger
methods.doc:
ResumeTrigger returns a paused trigger to the normal state, applying misfire
handling for any fires missed while paused.

methods.name: ScheduleJob
methods.doc:
ScheduleJob stores the job and its trigger and computes the trigger's first
fire time. If detail is nil the job is assumed to already exist in the store.

methods.name: Shutdown
methods.doc:
Shutdown stops the scheduling loop and the worker pool. When waitForJobs is
true it blocks until all in-flight and queued jobs have drained; otherwise it
cancels running jobs' contexts and returns promptly.

methods.name: Start
methods.doc:
Start launches the worker pool and the background scheduling loop. It is a
no-op if the scheduler is already running and returns ErrSchedulerShutdown if
it was shut down.

methods.name: Store
methods.doc: Store returns the underlying JobStore.

methods.name: TriggerJob
methods.doc:
TriggerJob fires a job immediately, out of band from its triggers, by
dispatching a one-off execution. The job must exist in the store.

methods.name: UnscheduleJob
methods.doc:
UnscheduleJob removes a trigger. If the referenced job is not durable and has
no remaining triggers, the job is removed as well.

types.name: SimpleScheduleBuilder
types.doc:
SimpleScheduleBuilder builds a SimpleTrigger: a start time plus a fixed
interval repeated a bounded number of times or forever. Create one with
SimpleSchedule.

funcs.name: SimpleSchedule
funcs.doc:
SimpleSchedule starts building a simple interval schedule. Without further
configuration it produces a trigger that fires exactly once at the start
time.

methods.name: BuildTrigger
methods.signature: func (b *SimpleScheduleBuilder) BuildTrigger(key, jobKey Key, start, end time.Time, description string) (Trigger, error)
methods.doc: BuildTrigger implements ScheduleBuilder.

methods.name: RepeatForever
methods.doc:
RepeatForever configures unbounded repeats and returns the builder for
chaining.

methods.name: WithInterval
methods.doc: WithInterval sets the repeat interval and returns the builder for chaining.

methods.name: WithIntervalInHours
methods.doc:
WithIntervalInHours sets the repeat interval in hours and returns the builder
for chaining.

methods.name: WithIntervalInMinutes
methods.doc:
WithIntervalInMinutes sets the repeat interval in minutes and returns the
builder for chaining.

methods.name: WithIntervalInSeconds
methods.doc:
WithIntervalInSeconds sets the repeat interval in seconds and returns the
builder for chaining.

methods.name: WithMisfirePolicy
methods.doc:
WithMisfirePolicy sets the misfire policy and returns the builder for
chaining.

methods.name: WithRepeatCount
methods.doc:
WithRepeatCount sets the number of repeats after the initial fire and returns
the builder for chaining. A count of N yields N+1 total fires.

types.name: SimpleTrigger
types.doc:
SimpleTrigger fires starting at a given time, then repeats on a fixed
interval either a bounded number of times or forever. A repeat count of N
results in N+1 total fires (the initial fire plus N repeats), matching the
semantics of the original Quartz project.

funcs.name: NewSimpleTrigger
funcs.signature: func NewSimpleTrigger(key, jobKey Key, start time.Time, interval time.Duration, repeatCount int) *SimpleTrigger
funcs.doc:
NewSimpleTrigger builds a SimpleTrigger. A repeatCount of RepeatForever means
unbounded repeats. An interval of zero with a non-zero repeat count is
invalid and will cause the trigger to fire only once.

methods.name: ComputeFirstFireTime
methods.doc: ComputeFirstFireTime implements Trigger.

methods.name: Description
methods.doc: Description implements Trigger.

methods.name: FireTimeAfter
methods.doc:
FireTimeAfter returns the first scheduled fire time strictly after the given
time, or the zero time when the trigger has no such fire (it is exhausted by
its repeat count or end time). It is a pure query that does not consult or
mutate the trigger's running state, mirroring the original Quartz
SimpleTrigger.getFireTimeAfter(Date). Fire times are the start time plus
whole multiples of the interval; a trigger with RepeatForever is unbounded,
otherwise it fires repeatCount+1 times in total.

methods.name: Interval
methods.doc: Interval returns the repeat interval.

methods.name: JobKey
methods.doc: JobKey implements Trigger.

methods.name: Key
methods.doc: Key implements Trigger.

methods.name: MisfirePolicy
methods.doc: MisfirePolicy implements Trigger.

methods.name: NextFireTime
methods.doc: NextFireTime implements Trigger.

methods.name: PreviousFireTime
methods.doc: PreviousFireTime implements Trigger.

methods.name: RepeatCount
methods.doc: RepeatCount returns the configured repeat count.

methods.name: StartTime
methods.doc: StartTime returns the configured start time.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns a debug representation of the trigger.

methods.name: TimesTriggered
methods.doc: TimesTriggered returns the number of times the trigger has fired.

methods.name: Triggered
methods.doc: Triggered implements Trigger.

methods.name: UpdateAfterMisfire
methods.doc:
UpdateAfterMisfire implements Trigger. It reschedules the trigger relative to
now according to its misfire policy.

methods.name: WillFireAgain
methods.doc: WillFireAgain implements Trigger.

methods.name: WithDescription
methods.doc: WithDescription sets the trigger description and returns the trigger.

methods.name: WithEndTime
methods.doc:
WithEndTime sets a time after which the trigger will not fire and returns the
trigger. The zero time means no end.

methods.name: WithMisfirePolicy
methods.doc: WithMisfirePolicy sets the misfire policy and returns the trigger.

types.name: StoredTrigger
types.doc: StoredTrigger couples a Trigger with its scheduling state inside a JobStore.

types.name: TimeOfDay
types.doc:
TimeOfDay is a wall-clock time-of-day, expressed as an hour, minute and
second offset from midnight. It carries no date or location; a
DailyTimeIntervalTrigger interprets it against each active day in the
trigger's own location so that window boundaries are wall-clock times.

funcs.name: NewTimeOfDay
funcs.signature: func NewTimeOfDay(h, m, s int) TimeOfDay
funcs.doc:
NewTimeOfDay builds a TimeOfDay from an hour, minute and second. The values
are stored as given; callers are responsible for supplying sane, normalized
components (for example 0-23 for the hour).

types.name: Trigger
types.doc:
Trigger describes when a job should fire. Implementations must be safe for
use by a single scheduler goroutine at a time; the scheduler serializes all
mutating calls (ComputeFirstFireTime and Triggered) per trigger.

types.name: TriggerBuilder
types.doc:
TriggerBuilder assembles a Trigger by combining an identity, a job reference,
a firing window and a ScheduleBuilder. Create one with NewTrigger.

funcs.name: NewTrigger
funcs.doc:
NewTrigger starts building a Trigger. Configure its identity, job and schedule
then call Build.

methods.name: Build
methods.doc:
Build produces the configured Trigger. It requires a job reference (set with
ForJob or ForJobDetail). When no identity was set a process-unique name is
generated. When no schedule was set a fire-once SimpleSchedule is used. When
no start time was set the trigger starts at the current time, matching the
original Quartz default.

methods.name: EndAt
methods.doc:
EndAt sets the latest time the trigger may fire and returns the builder for
chaining. The zero time means no end.

methods.name: ForJob
methods.doc:
ForJob sets the key of the job the trigger will fire and returns the builder
for chaining.

methods.name: ForJobDetail
methods.doc:
ForJobDetail sets the job reference from an existing JobDetail and returns the
builder for chaining.

methods.name: StartAt
methods.doc:
StartAt sets the earliest time the trigger may fire and returns the builder
for chaining.

methods.name: StartNow
methods.doc:
StartNow marks the trigger to begin at the moment Build is called and returns
the builder for chaining.

methods.name: WithDescription
methods.doc:
WithDescription sets the trigger's description and returns the builder for
chaining.

methods.name: WithIdentity
methods.doc: WithIdentity sets the trigger's key and returns the builder for chaining.

methods.name: WithIdentityName
methods.doc:
WithIdentityName sets the trigger's key to the given name in the default group
and returns the builder for chaining.

methods.name: WithIdentityNameGroup
methods.doc:
WithIdentityNameGroup sets the trigger's key to the given name and group and
returns the builder for chaining.

methods.name: WithSchedule
methods.doc:
WithSchedule sets the ScheduleBuilder that determines the trigger's firing
cadence and returns the builder for chaining.

types.name: TriggerListener
types.doc:
TriggerListener receives notifications about trigger firing. VetoJobExecution
allows a listener to prevent a job from running for a given fire.

types.name: TriggerState
types.signature: type TriggerState int
types.doc: TriggerState enumerates the scheduling state of a stored trigger.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns the trigger state's name (e.g. "waiting", "paused").

types.name: WeeklyCalendar
types.doc:
WeeklyCalendar excludes whole days of the week. By default Sunday and Saturday
are excluded. Exclusions have day granularity and the weekday is determined in
the calendar's location.

funcs.name: NewWeeklyCalendar
funcs.doc: NewWeeklyCalendar returns a WeeklyCalendar with Sunday and Saturday excluded.

methods.name: IsTimeIncluded
methods.doc:
IsTimeIncluded reports whether t's weekday is not excluded and the base
Calendar also includes t.

methods.name: NextIncludedTime
methods.doc:
NextIncludedTime returns the earliest instant at or after t that falls on a
non-excluded weekday and is allowed by the base Calendar.

methods.name: SetDayExcluded
methods.doc: SetDayExcluded sets whether the given weekday is excluded.

funcs.name: RegisterJobType
funcs.signature: func RegisterJobType(name string, factory func() Job)
funcs.doc:
RegisterJobType registers a factory that constructs a fresh Job of a concrete
type. The name must match the value returned by jobTypeName for instances of
that type (that is, reflect.TypeOf(job).String(), such as
"*mypkg.EmailJob"), because the SQL store persists a job by that name and
rebuilds it by looking the factory up again. Registering the same name twice
replaces the previous factory. It is safe for concurrent use.

examples.output:
next fire: 2026-01-01 10:00:00
fired at 09:59: 0
report generated
fired at 10:00: 1
examples.doc:
Example demonstrates scheduling a cron job and firing it deterministically
with an injected clock. Because the clock is controlled by the test, the
scheduler can be driven with ProcessDue instead of sleeping.

examples.name: SimpleTrigger
examples.doc:
ExampleSimpleTrigger shows a bounded, repeating interval trigger and its fire
times computed with an injected clock.
